summary: Add final local smoke coverage and release-readiness documentation for the 48-hour MVP without performing a public deployment.
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic local smoke test or no-network test path exists for the MVP happy path: radar task context, mock scan producing at least one opportunity, opportunity detail readiness, and MVP Spec generation readiness. It must not require real providers, secrets, network access, a running browser, or public deployment.

README documents the final local validation sequence for a fresh developer checkout, including environment setup, database validation/generation/migration/seed commands, tests, build, dev server, and the manual demo flow.
README and .env.example distinguish required local values from optional future provider, email, payment, cron, and deployment values, without adding real secrets.

The deployment-preparation notes make clear that public deployment, production auth/session/ownership policy, paid provider setup, CI/CD, and commercial release decisions remain human-gated and are not executed in this task.

If package.json is changed, it may add only no-dependency convenience scripts that wrap existing test/build commands; no dependencies, package-lock changes, or runtime behavior changes are introduced.
Focused automated coverage, npm test, npm run build, taskctl validate, and git diff --check pass.
notes: Derived from docs/09_codex_implementation_tasks.md Task 15, docs/11_development_plan.md M6 final acceptance, and docs/10_acceptance_criteria_and_test_plan.md MVP completion definition.
This task is deployment readiness, not deployment. Actual public deployment, production secrets, accounts, DNS, CI/CD, auth/session policy, and commercial release remain human-gated.
security_reviewer is required because README/.env guidance touches secrets and deployment posture, even though no real secrets or deployment action should be added.
Keep the task focused on confidence and documentation. If the smoke test exposes a product bug outside the allowed paths, stop with REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCKED and propose a separate scoped fix task.
non_goals: No actual Vercel/self-hosted deployment, public release, DNS, GitHub Actions, CI/CD, store listing, paid provider setup, or account creation.

No new package dependency, package-lock change, Prisma schema/migration/seed change, database reset, destructive data action, auth/session/ownership implementation, billing, telemetry, public sharing, cron, or email sending.
No changes to agents, services, lib runtime code, app routes, components, scoring, scan orchestration, provider adapters, API handlers, or generated product behavior.
No broad README rewrite beyond final local validation, smoke, environment, and deployment-readiness sections.
